segments.text: Mona: Hello Tom, sorry I'm late.
Tom: Hi Mona, no problem.
Tom: I heard you've finished the essay for the management course.
Tom: Which company did you focus on?
Mona: It was finished last week.
Mona: I chose the hotel where I interned last summer and mainly concentrated on my work experience there.
Tom: Could you give me some details?
Tom: I'm interested.
Mona: Well, during my three-month internship, I observed every part of the hotel carefully each day and also talked with some of my colleagues.
Mona: I've found some problems in different parts of the hotel and made a conclusion in the essay.
Mona: Let me start from the reception desk.
Mona: I noticed the staff there kept doing the same thing every day, like answering the phone, helping guests with check-in and check-out, etc.
Mona: There was nothing special for them.
Tom: That must be very boring.
Mona: Yeah I think so.
Mona: And in the restaurant, the employees told me that they must obey the rules exactly.
Mona: For example, if they don't wear uniforms during working hours, 20% of their daily wages will be deducted.
Mona: And then about the coffee bar.
Mona: During peak hours, I was asked to help in the coffee bar several times, as a shortage of hands always made many customers wait long for ordering, eating or paying the bills.
Mona: Their own staff could not control customer flow.
Tom: Oh, that seems a little messy.
Mona: Yes, definitely.
Mona: And the place where I worked was a shop selling towels, and pyjamas as well as souvenirs.
Mona: Every day I had to be there from 9 am to 6 pm.
Mona: My duty was to set up the window display and serve the customers.
Mona: At first it seemed interesting, but later I found this work was a bit tough.
Mona: Every night when I got off work, I was nearly exhausted.
Mona: What I wanted was just a good rest and sleep.
Tom: Doesn't sound like an easy job.
Mona: It definitely wasn't.
Mona: Then there was the personnel office.
Mona: I went there on the first day when I arrived at the hotel.
Mona: As I was not familiar with everything, I asked the staff several questions.
Mona: But the employee of the personnel office seemed a bit annoyed and started complaining, which gave me a very bad impression.

Mona: Lastly, as regards cleaning, it wasn't too bad compared with other parts of the hotel, except the vacuum cleaners, which were probably bought such a long time ago that the sound seemed too loud when they were working.
Mona: But fortunately, the hotel hasn't received any complaint from the customers so far.
Tom: I think you've benefited a lot from the internship.
Mona: Yeah, absolutely.
Mona: In the beginning, I just wanted to complete the essay for the course.

Mona: But in the end, I found it has given me some practical examples which are helpful to what I intend to do after graduation, as you know I've always wanted to do something related to hotel management.
Mona: Due to the time limitation, I still haven't got a clear idea of some specific work positions.
Tom: Well, I heard you're going to give us a presentation of your essay next time in class?
Mona: Yes, actually I'm not so good at giving a presentation.

Mona: But the tutor says that's just what he wants me to do, to know what is important when doing a presentation, like how to attract the audience, make eye contact and make gestures, etc., and other students can learn from my presentation.
Tom: I'm looking forward to it.
Tom: You know, now I'm only in the second year of the course, and I have to write an essay next semester.
Tom: But I have no idea about where I can do an internship.
Tom: There're several hotels which I'm interested in.
Tom: Do you think I should call their managers?
Mona: Well, I suggest you'd better not because you're more likely to be rejected by them.
Mona: There is a career office on campus that has got a list of companies offering internship opportunities to students.
Mona: The office will help you contact where you want to go.
Mona: You just have to prepare your personal information like the CV.
Mona: Oh, don't forget to provide a reference from the tutor.
Tom: Great!
Tom: I've got it.
Mona: So, have you got any plans for your essay?
Tom: I've noticed that the hotel industry is developing at a high speed.
Tom: People are more and more concerned about the quality of accommodation.
Tom: What I'm struck by is the numerous statistics showing the ever-increasing number of five-star hotels, so I intend to carry out extensive research on this aspect.
Tom: I'm thinking about using video as a research method, but I haven't decided yet.
Mona: I think it's an interesting topic.
Mona: Anyway, if you have any questions later, I will be happy to help.
Tom: Thanks a lot, Mona.
Mona: You're welcome.
